subject: Chemistry
title: Sodium: The Explosive Metal
description: A quick, practical guide to Sodium: The Explosive Metal.

hook.text: Drop a chunk of sodium in water and it bursts into flames, skittering across the surface like a fireball.

buildup.text: Sodium is a soft, silvery metal you can cut with a knife. It tarnishes in seconds in open air.

discovery.text: It reacts violently with water because it desperately wants to lose its single outer electron.

twist.text: Despite the drama, sodium is essential for life. Your nerves use sodium ions to send every signal.

climax.text: Table salt is sodium chloride—this explosive metal bonded to a toxic gas makes food taste better.

punchline.text: Explosive in water, essential in your body. That's sodium.

quiz.question: Why does sodium react violently with water?
options: It desperately wants to lose its single outer electron
It absorbs water and expands rapidly
